name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: New Registry Added to HKLM/HKCU (Windows)
notes: This Building Block detects new registry key set under HKLM or HKCU directories. While this activity only is legitimate, if there are potential ransomware behaviors detected this may be a persistence method.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: New Registry Added to HKLM/HKCU (Windows) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification
when the event matches LOWER("Registry Key") MATCHES 'hku\\[^\\]*\\.*?$' OR LOWER("Registry Key") MATCHES 'hklm\\software\\[^\\]*?$' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: New Registry Added to HKLM/HKCU (Windows) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification
AND when the event matches LOWER("Registry Key") MATCHES 'hku\\[^\\]*\\.*?$' OR LOWER("Registry Key") MATCHES 'hklm\\software\\[^\\]*?$' AQL filter query
